findings.title: First-tool latency / discoverability impact of ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH=true
findings.rationale: Discoverability impact is source-supported: the setting was non-obvious and the author only found it after inspecting tool-schema overhead. First-tool latency and task ergonomics remain unmeasured, so the gap narrows but

findings.title: Edit-retry root-cause attribution across prompt | workflow | guardrail buckets
findings.rationale: UNKNOWN. After the doc reread and the source reread, the only recoverable fact is that 31 edit-retry chains existed. There is no evidence to bucket them into prompt, workflow, or guardrail causes without fabricating.

findings.title: Net-cost audit of /clear + plugin-memory remedy bundle vs stale-resume baseline
findings.rationale: The bundle's components are now clearer, but the net-cost question still is not answered. The source names the compensating mechanisms behind /clear, yet never counts the extra injected tokens, hook writes, or recall cos

findings.title: Reddit-post arithmetic reconciliation
findings.rationale: The sub-math is mixed. 264M tokens is locally consistent as a rounded 14,000*22*858 estimate, and the 54% idle-gap ratio matches 6,152/11,357. But the post still never reconciles the 926-session header or why 11,357, not

findings.title: Cross-phase validation of phase 005 auditor/parser vs F14-F17 expectations
findings.rationale: Closed at the design-contract level. Phase 5's shipped research matches F14-F17: it defines an offline JSONL-to-analytics auditor, keeps parsing behind a guarded replay model, rejects raw low-usage disable thresholds as

findings.title: Empirical validation of the 11.2x token claim
findings.rationale: There is no empirical Claude/GPT validation in this checkout. The 11.2x headline is a README benchmark built from heuristic token math, while the shipped eval harness validates detector accuracy only. The honest closure

findings.title: tRPC / Fastify contract enrichment despite AST routing
findings.rationale: Closed as a real implementation gap. tRPC gets AST route discovery but no contract pass at all. Fastify is not fully missing, but it only receives the same generic Hono-skewed regex enrichment as several other TS framewo

findings.title: Go regex+brace-tracking labeled confidence: ast
findings.rationale: This is a mislabel, not pending AST support. The implementation is a structured regex parser constrained by a binary confidence enum, and it currently advertises that result as ast. The honest fix would be a third label

findings.title: Monorepo support beyond pnpm + npm workspaces
findings.rationale: Support is genuinely limited to npm-style workspaces and pnpm-workspace manifests. That creates a real omission risk for turbo, nx, lerna, or Rush repos that do not mirror workspace membership into those files. The silen

findings.title: Blast-radius BFS leak and heuristic schema-impact false-positive rate
findings.rationale: The failure modes are source-confirmed, so the qualitative risk is real: depth can leak one hop too far and schema impact can over-fire whenever an affected file owns any db-tagged route. But the actual false-positive ra

findings.title: 93% token reduction is estimated from fixed constants, not benchmarked against Public's typed stack
findings.rationale: The 93% figure is not a benchmark. It is synthetic arithmetic over two hard-coded 25k-token constants plus recorded usage counters, and the repo does not contain any side-by-side task benchmark against Public's CocoIndex

findings.title: Mainframe privacy model is weakened by public_chat room creation and plaintext local credentials
findings.rationale: Closed negatively: the privacy weakness is real, not speculative. Mainframe stores reusable local credentials in plaintext, shares rich query metadata in room history, and uses a room preset that weakens the intent of pr

findings.title: Matrix room-state writes use optimistic blind updates with no atomic conflict semantics
findings.rationale: The contention question closes negatively. Mainframe has no atomic lock or merge protocol: budget and janitor state are optimistic read-modify-write records, so concurrent agents can overwrite each other or both proceed

findings.title: GitHub webhook automation lacks idempotency tracking, secret rotation, and routed failure notificati
findings.rationale: Closed negatively at the source level. The webhook flow is deterministic enough to understand, but it is not hardened: no idempotency keying, no secret-rotation lane, and no explicit out-of-band failure notification beyo

findings.title: Repair queue has no completion guarantee after regeneration failures
findings.rationale: The repair queue does not provide completion guarantees. It offers eventual reattempt opportunity, not eventual success: failures are carried forward, some paths degrade to stubs or stamps, and nothing enforces bounded r

findings.title: 71.5x token reduction is heuristic math, not Anthropic count_tokens-grounded
findings.rationale: The 71.5x number remains a heuristic, not a token-API-grounded benchmark. Phase 4 was correct to treat it as directionally interesting but not credibility-grade evidence for Public without count_tokens validation.

findings.title: Graphify cache has monotonic growth, mtime-only incremental detection, and no deletion tombstones
findings.rationale: Closed negatively. Graphify does have a useful content-hash cache layer, but the maintenance story is weak: cache entries accumulate indefinitely, deletion is all-or-nothing, and incremental detection has no deletion or

findings.title: Cross-language extraction parity is uneven and Swift is detected but never extracted
findings.rationale: Closed. The parity gap is structural, not anecdotal: Python has the only rich extractor surface, the other 11 extractors are materially thinner, and Swift support is advertised at detection time but absent from both file

findings.title: INFERRED confidence-score inconsistency does not drive built-in ranking, but it leaks into reports a
findings.rationale: The inconsistency is real, but it does not currently harm graphify's built-in ranking quality. Ranking and surprise ordering use categorical confidence only; the mixed 0.5 vs 0.4-0.9 numerics mainly affect exported `grap

findings.title: mixed-corpus multimodal claim is not supported by the checked-in worked artifact
findings.rationale: The artifact-level issue is real: the checked-in `mixed-corpus` report is stale or mislabeled relative to the narrative around it. What remains unclosed is whether a fresh rerun would show the promised multimodal nodes;

findings.title: PreToolUse Glob|Grep nudge effectiveness is still unmeasured
findings.rationale: UNKNOWN. The repo proves the hook mechanism and the intended behavioral nudge, but it contains no measured evidence that Claude actually changes tool-selection behavior or improves outcomes because of it.

findings.title: Claudest truly supports PRAGMA-based FTS4 fallback, but Public's safe v1 remains FTS5 BM25 to LIKE
findings.rationale: Closed. Claudest's own pattern is real: it probes SQLite capabilities and can create either FTS5 or FTS4 tables. That strengthens phase 5's narrowed conclusion that Public's smallest safe v1 is `fts5_bm25 -> like_scan` u

findings.title: Stop hook still omits transcript_path persistence, cache-token carry-forward, and turn-level offsets
findings.rationale: Closed negatively. Phase 5's continuation docs already resolved the ambiguity: Public's current Stop hook is not yet a trustworthy producer for normalized analytics, and Brief B remains blocked until that producer metada

findings.title: The claude-memory plugin-specific CLAUDE.md is absent in this checkout
findings.rationale: Closed. The anomaly stands: there is no plugin-local `claude-memory/CLAUDE.md` to inspect in this checkout, so any per-plugin layer model still has to be reconstructed from the root CLAUDE, README material, and skill/age

findings.title: Fallback-context docstring contradicts the implemented first-2 plus last-6 renderer
findings.rationale: Closed. The drift is real and source-proven: the docstring is stale, but the actual fallback behavior matches the summarizer's full-or-first/last rendering scheme rather than a last-3-exchanges view.

findings.title: Public's 008-signal-extraction packet is a monolithic extractor unification, not an auditor/discover
findings.rationale: Closed. Public's `008-signal-extraction` packet is a monolithic extractor-unification effort: one canonical `SemanticSignalExtractor` plus thin adapters. It does not already embody Claudest's verifier/discoverer split, s

findings.title: Single-canonical-ScanResult orchestration shape
findings.rationale: Assumes one upstream scan feeding multiple surfaces; clashes with explicit split routing.

findings.title: AST-first / regex-fallback / confidence labels
findings.rationale: Single-surface structural improvement; no semantic/memory overlap.

findings.title: Per-tool profile overlay split
findings.rationale: Static assistant-profile generation sits beside the three core surfaces.

findings.title: Static artifacts as default + MCP as overlay
findings.rationale: Adds a packaging layer above existing surfaces and needs careful source-of-truth boundaries.

findings.title: F1 fixture harness for detector regression
findings.rationale: Test-only utility; zero routing conflict.

findings.title: Hot-file ranking by incoming import count
findings.rationale: Fits structural analysis only if named honestly as degree count.

findings.title: SQLAlchemy AST schema extraction
findings.rationale: New detector/schema work inside one surface; no cross-surface ownership conflict.

findings.title: Config-gated bootstrap layering
findings.rationale: Pushes toward one bootstrap facade over all three substrates; risks turning split routing into a monolith.

findings.title: Generated `.mcp.json` scaffold plus setup hints
findings.rationale: Activation ergonomics only; no schema conflict with core surfaces.

findings.title: Benchmark-honest token reporting
findings.rationale: Measurement rubric layers cleanly onto existing telemetry/reporting.

findings.title: Evidence-tagging contract + `confidence_score`
findings.rationale: Additive schema extension across two read surfaces; safe if tiers remain surface-local.

findings.title: Graph-first PreToolUse hook
findings.rationale: Nudges the model toward the existing split instead of replacing it.

findings.title: Two-layer cache invalidation
findings.rationale: Cross-surface freshness policy work, but still preserves separate owners and indexes.

findings.title: CLAUDE.md companion section pattern
findings.rationale: Guidance layer only; composes by reinforcing split routing.

findings.title: `validate.py` schema-boundary validator
findings.rationale: Local validation layer at interchange boundary; no routing conflict.

findings.title: Leiden clustering
findings.rationale: Optional structural overlay; one-surface change with tunable params needed from day one.

findings.title: Modality-aware rebuild policy layer
findings.rationale: Coordinates freshness across multiple surfaces and can blur who owns rebuild state.

findings.title: Stable JSON interchange artifact
findings.rationale: Adds a durable projection boundary, but needs provenance ownership rules.

findings.title: Runtime FTS capability cascade
findings.rationale: Single-surface storage/search change, but it touches schema, migration, and query fallback behavior.

findings.title: Cached `context_summary` SessionStart fast path
findings.rationale: Direct fit for continuity/recovery surface; no semantic or structural overlap.

findings.title: Deterministic summary computation at Stop time
findings.rationale: Producer-side improvement inside the same memory pipeline.

findings.title: Auditor vs discoverer split
findings.rationale: Reorganizes memory-quality workflow inside one surface; no cross-surface conflict, but non-trivial pipeline split.

findings.title: 4-phase consolidation contract
findings.rationale: Workflow scaffold for existing memory review flows.

findings.title: Per-tier pricing and cache normalization
findings.rationale: Analytics schema extension within one surface; moderate implementation cost, low topology risk.

findings.title: Cache-cliff metric
findings.rationale: Observability metric on top of existing session/telemetry data.

findings.title: Dashboard JSON contracts
findings.rationale: Presentation contract only if HTML is not imported wholesale.

findings.title: Placement rubric for memory consolidation
findings.rationale: Policy layer over existing memory destinations, not a new retrieval surface.

findings.title: Search/browse separation
findings.rationale: Strengthens one surface API and already partly matches Public’s mode split.

findings.title: Public should only publish token-savings headlines from frozen, quality-gated tasks
findings.rationale: Q-A converged on one publication rule: frozen tasks, answer-quality gates, and provider-counted totals before any headline multiplier.

findings.title: Public already leads structural and semantic query, so adoption should reinforce routing
findings.rationale: The capability matrix shows Public leading the two retrieval lanes that matter most for split-topology work, so imported ideas should strengthen routing and packaging rather than replace the substrate.

findings.title: Monolithic scan or bootstrap facades are the main architecture risk in this packet
findings.rationale: Q-C found only three high-risk candidates, and all three tried to sit above semantic, structural, and memory ownership at once.

findings.title: P0 should front-load measurement, producer summaries, and schema discipline before feature scale
findings.rationale: Q-D sequences rails first because they unlock later work without forcing topology changes.

findings.title: Contextador is a concept-transfer-only reference because AGPL and Bun are both real gates
findings.rationale: Q-E makes the AGPL/Bun boundary explicit: study the patterns, but do not borrow source, fixtures, or program-shaped prompts.

findings.title: The strongest combo is warm-start memory plus graph-first routing, not a new super-surface
findings.rationale: Q-F ranks the cached-summary plus graph-first-routing combination highest because it improves startup and first-query behavior while preserving Public’s split.

findings.title: ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH is already the baseline win in Public
findings.rationale: The first-pass conclusion that deferred tool loading is already a baseline win remains intact and was never contradicted later.

findings.title: generateAIConfigs ships one shared summary into 5 assistant files with safe append semantics
findings.rationale: CodeSight’s per-tool overlay generation remained reusable across the whole consolidation and reappears in both Q-C and Q-D as a clean packaging pattern.

findings.title: Bootstrap is root-scoped, config-driven, and keeps Mainframe optional
findings.rationale: Contextador’s bootstrap ergonomics survived as concept-transfer guidance even though the source itself remains legally and operationally blocked.

findings.title: Evidence labeling is a transport-level guarantee, not a UI convention
findings.rationale: Graphify’s evidence-tagging contract stayed central to the composition study and remains a reusable pattern if Public keeps confidence local to each surface.

findings.title: Cached summary fast path turns startup recall into a DB lookup
findings.rationale: Claudest’s cached-summary fast path stayed valid throughout the run and became the highest-ranked combo ingredient as well as a P1 roadmap item.
